data.content:
Plan summary for `plan_review`:

ScopeData domain (src/domain/scope.rs:49-71) already models every acceptance
field as Option<…> and is projected through KnotView, KnotCacheRecord, events,
sync, and CLI. The plumbing landed in knots-5091, knots-7dda, knots-ee7d.

The implementation step for this knot is verification-only:
1. cargo test passes (full suite, incl. tests_scope_events, tests_scope,
   apply_tests_legacy_defaults).
2. kno -C ~/knots fsck validates the store.
3. Spot-check the 10 acceptance fields against ScopeData; spot-check
   KnotView.scope / scope_json projection match.

No new production code expected unless a gap is found, in which case extend
ScopeData (+ apply_to, event payload, migration shape if needed) and add a
unit test.

Naming note: acceptance text uses `scope_volume`, `scope_reliability`, etc.;
domain elides the `scope_` prefix because the type is `ScopeData`. Treat
them as semantically equivalent.

Volume vs. estimate: no pre-existing knot estimate/minutes field exists;
`minutes` lives only in lease_expiry, `estimate` appears only in the
scope_volume CLI help text. No conflict.

Risks: low. Plan is essentially a pass-through verification.
